title: Changelog
text: notable changes project documented .

title: Changelog
text: step_timer_expire(people, from_state, to_state) — transition absorbing (untimed) state; timer left 0 arrival (e.g. →S, R→S). step_timer_expire_set(people, from_state, to_state, duration_dist) — transition state duration; fresh per-agent timer drawn duration_dist arrival (e.g. E→, →R waning). four named kernels (step_exposed_ei, step_infectious_ir, step_infectious_is, step_recovered_rs) now thin, fixed-state wrappers two helpers, shared decrement/transition loop lives one place. Covered tests/testthat/test-timer-kernels.R, proves wrappers equivalent generalized kernels. dist_normal(mean, variance) — Gaussian (second argument variance σ², standard deviation). dist_constant(value) — degenerate distribution; fixed-value drop-. dist_uniform(low, high) — continuous uniform [low, high). dist_gamma(shape, scale) — gamma shape–scale (k, θ) parameterization (mean shape*scale, variance shape*scale^2); strictly positive draws. dist_poisson(lambda) — Poisson rate/mean lambda; non-negative integer counts. dist_beta(alpha, beta) — beta (0, 1) shape parameters α, β. dist_exp(rate) — exponential rate λ (mean 1/rate). dist_logistic(location, scale) — logistic (mean location, variance scale^2·π²/3); sampled inverse-CDF transform. dist_lognormal(meanlog, sdlog) — log-normal log-space parameters (matches R’s qlnorm). Distribution$sample_one() Distribution$sample_n(n) — draw one / batch floating-point samples thread-local RNG (interactive use, testing, statistical validation). draws doubles. Internal Rust sampler Distribution::sample (Pattern B): caller supplies RNG, one &Distribution can shared reference sampled concurrently across Rayon worker threads. tests/testthat/test-distributions.R covering constructors use step kernels, tests/testthat/test-distributions-validation.R validating parameter wiring R’s reference implementations (qnorm, qunif, qgamma, qpois/dpois) one million draws. examples/sir_attack_fraction.R examples/seir_attack_fraction.R — runnable SIR SEIR examples plot compartment trajectories compare simulated final attack fraction Kermack–McKendrick final-size relation = 1 - exp(-R0 * ) across R0 sweep, timing output (examples/README.md sample output plots). CLAUDE.md documenting downstream-first transition-ordering convention composing models step kernels.

title: Changelog
text: Model compositions (SIR/SEIR/SEIRS test helpers examples) now call per-tick transitions downstream-first (timed compartment : R→S, →R, E→, S→E) exposed/infectious periods reflect full configured duration rather shortened one tick. See CLAUDE.md. Distributions return floating-point values; integer rounding state timers now lives step kernels (shared duration_ticks helper rounds nearest tick clamps minimum 1). Distribution longer exposes integer sample_duration. step_transmission_si(people, nodes, beta, inf_dist) — inf_duration (sets timer S→). step_transmission_se(people, nodes, beta, exp_dist) — exp_duration (sets E timer S→E). step_exposed_ei(people, inf_dist) — inf_duration (sets timer E→). step_infectious_ir(people, imm_dist) — imm_duration (sets R waning timer →R; use dist_constant(0) SIR/SEIR R timer never read). Steps transitioning untimed states (step_infectious_is, step_recovered_rs, step_mortality_cdr, step_births_cbr) unchanged. SI/SIR/SEIR/SEIRS test helpers updated wrap durations dist_constant().

title: razer — project notes for Claude
text: composing model per-tick step kernels, call transitions downstream-first order — .e. move agents timed compartment moving agents , walking disease-progression chain backwards: . timed transition sets fresh duration timer agent enters state, step next transition decrements timer. inflow step runs outflow step within tick, just-arrived agent decremented immediately loses one tick residency — effective exposed/infectious period duration − 1 instead duration. Running downstream-first (outflow inflow) prevents : newly exposed newly infectious agent touched next tick. validated attack-fraction examples: downstream-first ordering SEIR model matches Kermack–McKendrick final size = 1 − exp(−R0·) R0 = beta · D (full infectious period D). SIR caveat. direct S→transmission offset persists regardless order: agent enters inside step_transmission_si, step’s infectious tally computed, first contributes force infection next tick. SIR effective reproduction number therefore R0 = beta · (D − 1), beta · D. (SEIR avoids agents enter via step_exposed_ei, separate step runs transmission tally.) See examples/sir_attack_fraction.R examples/seir_attack_fraction.R. Steps transition untimed states (step_infectious_is, step_recovered_rs S; step_mortality_cdr D; step_births_cbr) suffer artifact, step_recovered_rs still outflow R leads SEIRS.

title: razer — project notes for Claude
text: cargo ~/.cargo/bin (default PATH); prepend export PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" cargo / rextendr / R CMD INSTALL. changing Rust signatures, regenerate R wrappers, NAMESPACE, man pages Rscript -e 'rextendr::document()' package root. Distribution constructors use dist_ prefix (dist_normal, dist_gamma, …) avoid masking base/stats functions base::gamma / stats::poisson. Run tests Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'.

title: razer — project notes for Claude
text: Integrate fast-forward — never create merge commit. Merge feature worktree branch main git merge --ff-<branch>; branch diverged, rebase onto main first, fast-forward. Enforced repo-locally via merge.ff = pull.ff = git config.

what: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Rust-backed Agent modeling Zero-copy struct--arrays Eradication Research razer R interface LASER (Light Agent Spatial modeling ERadication) toolkit. provides high-performance, Rust-backed data structures large-scale spatial agent-based disease models. name deliberate pun: “raze” means eradicate completely, “razor” echoes LASER, trailing R anchors R.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: LASER Python framework developed Institute Disease Modeling building fast, composable agent-based disease models national global scale. core design insight struct--arrays (SoA) memory layout: instead one object per agent, agent property flat array length capacity, active agents contiguous slice array. layout cache-friendly vectorised, Numba-JIT-compiled kernels drive LASER’s performance. razer ports memory model R, implementing backing arrays Rust (via extendr framework) exposing LaserFrame object mirrors laser.core.LaserFrame Python package.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Install Rust don’t already : R package dependencies (installed automatically install.packages devtools::install()):

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: first install triggers full Cargo build; subsequent installs incremental much faster.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
code: library(razer)  # Create a frame: 1 000 agents, all active from the start pop <- LaserFrame$new(1000L, -1L)  # Register properties (one value per agent) pop$add_scalar_property("age",   "integer", 0L) pop$add_scalar_property("alive", "logical", TRUE)  # $ shorthand: read and write scalar properties directly pop$age   <- sample(0:80, 1000L, replace = TRUE) pop$alive <- sample(c(TRUE, FALSE), 1000L, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.99, 0.01))  # Activate 50 more agents (capacity must allow it) large_pop <- LaserFrame$new(2000L, 1000L) large_pop$add_scalar_property("age", "integer", 0L) range <- large_pop$add(50L)                   # returns c(1001L, 1050L) large_pop$age[range[1]:range[2]] <- sample(20:40, 50L, replace = TRUE)  # Vector property: capacity × nticks, column-major #   $S returns the full (count × ncols) matrix; $get_col("S", tick) is faster #   for single-tick access (one contiguous copy, no column gathering) nticks <- 365L pop$add_vector_property("S", nticks, "integer", 0L) pop$set_col("S", 1L, rep(950L, pop$count))    # initial susceptibles  # Compact: remove dead agents pop$squash(pop$alive)                         # in-place; count updated  # Sort agents by age (Rayon-parallel across all scalar properties) pop$sort_by(order(pop$age))  # Describe the frame layout cat(pop$describe())

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: LaserFrame uses extendr environment-based dispatch: create instance LaserFrame$new(...), call methods $.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Properties may added twice name.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Method names take priority property names — property named count, use f$get("count") explicitly.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Scalar properties — backing array length capacity; active slice [1:count]. Vector properties — backing array capacity × length elements stored column-major (R’s native order). Element [entry, col] lives byte offset entry + col * capacity. Column col (active entries one time step) contiguous block, making get_col single memcpy.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Beyond LaserFrame data structure, razer provides composable per-tick step kernels building SI / SIR / SEIR / SEIRS-family compartmental models, together parameterized distributions (dist_normal(), dist_gamma(), dist_poisson(), …) drawing state-timer durations incubation infectious periods. See function reference complete list.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Call transitions downstream-first within tick — move agents timed compartment moving agents , walking disease-progression chain backwards: keeps compartment’s residence time equal configured duration: newly exposed infectious agent otherwise decremented tick arrives, shortening period tick. See model-composition help topic (?\model-composition``) full rationale SIR caveat.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: examples/ directory two runnable scripts build model, plot trajectories, validate simulated final attack fraction classic Kermack–McKendrick final-size relation = 1 − exp(−R0·). match theory within thousandths across supercritical range. Run either , e.g., Rscript examples/sir_attack_fraction.R. sir_attack_fraction.R — SIR model  seir_attack_fraction.R — SEIR model  See examples/README.md details, including effective R0 differs SIR (beta·(D−1)) SEIR (beta·D).

what: Composing models: order of per-tick update operations — model-composition
title: Composing models: order of per-tick update operations — model-composition
text: Guidance assembling model per-tick step kernels (step_transmission_si(), step_transmission_se(), step_exposed_ei(), step_infectious_ir(), step_infectious_is(), step_recovered_rs()): within tick, call transitions downstream-first — move agents timed compartment moving agents , walking disease-progression chain backwards.

title: Composing models: order of per-tick update operations — model-composition
text: Recommended per-tick call order, model: SIR step_infectious_ir(), step_transmission_si() SEIR step_infectious_ir(), step_exposed_ei(), step_transmission_se() SEIRS step_recovered_rs(), step_infectious_ir(), step_exposed_ei(), step_transmission_se() SIS step_infectious_is(), step_transmission_si() matters. timed transition sets fresh duration timer agent enters state, step next transition decrements timer. inflow step runs outflow step within tick, just-arrived agent decremented immediately loses one tick residency — effective exposed infectious period becomes duration - 1 rather duration. Running downstream-first prevents : newly exposed infectious agent touched next tick. SIR caveat. direct S -> transmission one-tick offset persists regardless order. agent becomes infectious inside step_transmission_si(), step already computed infectious tally, first contributes force infection next tick. SIR effective basic reproduction number therefore beta * (D - 1), whereas SEIR — agents enter via separate step_exposed_ei(), runs transmission tally — yields full beta * D. sir_attack_fraction.R seir_attack_fraction.R scripts package's examples/ directory demonstrate , validating simulated attack fraction Kermack-McKendrick final-size relation. Generalized timer kernels. four named timer transitions fixed-state shorthands two generalized kernels parameterized /state codes — use directly add transitions named kernels cover: step_timer_expire()(people, , ) transition absorbing (untimed) state; timer left 0. Generalizes step_infectious_is() (->S) step_recovered_rs() (R->S). step_timer_expire_set()(people, , , dist) transition state duration; fresh per-agent timer drawn dist. Generalizes step_exposed_ei() (E->) step_infectious_ir() (->R).

what: Stochastic birth step using a crude birth rate. — step_births_cbr
title: Stochastic birth step using a crude birth rate. — step_births_cbr
text: Parallelism: Bernoulli draw (agent give birth?) parallelised across active agents. subsequent bookkeeping — incrementing count writing new-agent properties — serial modifies frame's active count.

title: Stochastic birth step using a crude birth rate. — step_births_cbr
Stochastic birth step using a crude birth rate. — step_births_cbr
text: people LaserFrame agents. cbr Crude birth rate per agent per tick (probability [0, 1]).

title: Stochastic birth step using a crude birth rate. — step_births_cbr
text: active (non-D) agent, draws Bernoulli(cbr) birth event. birth creates one new agent inherits parent's node starts state S timer = 0. scalar properties new agents take default value set add_scalar_property time. Excess births beyond capacity - count silently dropped. Required people properties: state, node, timer (integer).

title: Timer-based E→I transition (SEIR kernel). — step_exposed_ei
text: exposed agent, decrements timer 1. timer reaches 0 agent transitions state timer set fresh draw inf_dist, infectious-period distribution. Pass dist_constant(d) fixed period d ticks, e.g. dist_normal(mean, variance) stochastic per-agent period. Draws rounded nearest tick clamped minimum 1.

title: Timer-based E→I transition (SEIR kernel). — step_exposed_ei
Timer-based E→I transition (SEIR kernel). — step_exposed_ei
text: people LaserFrame agents. inf_dist Distribution (e.g. dist_constant() dist_normal()) giving infectious period ticks; sampled written timer E→.

title: Timer-based E→I transition (SEIR kernel). — step_exposed_ei
text: fixed-state shorthand step_timer_expire_set()(people, E, , inf_dist). RNG: thread-local — Rayon worker draws thread_rng (Pattern B: kernel owns RNG passes sampler). single inf_dist handle shared across threads reference.

what: Timer-based I→R transition (SIR / SEIR / SEIRS kernel). — step_infectious_ir
title: Timer-based I→R transition (SIR / SEIR / SEIRS kernel). — step_infectious_ir
text: infectious agent, decrements timer 1. timer reaches 0 agent transitions state R timer set draw imm_dist, immunity (waning) period.

title: Timer-based I→R transition (SIR / SEIR / SEIRS kernel). — step_infectious_ir
Timer-based I→R transition (SIR / SEIR / SEIRS kernel). — step_infectious_ir
text: people LaserFrame agents. imm_dist Distribution (e.g. dist_constant() dist_normal()) giving immunity period ticks; sampled written timer →R (rounded whole ticks, clamped minimum 1). Use dist_constant(0) SIR / SEIR.

title: Timer-based I→R transition (SIR / SEIR / SEIRS kernel). — step_infectious_ir
text: fixed-state shorthand step_timer_expire_set()(people, , R, imm_dist). SEIRS, pass desired immunity distribution step_recovered_rs counts R→S fresh per-agent draw. SIR SEIR (waning, step_recovered_rs never called) R timer never read, distribution works — dist_constant(0) conventional "care". RNG: thread-local — Rayon worker draws thread_rng (Pattern B). single imm_dist handle shared across threads reference.

what: Timer-based I→S transition for SIS models (no immunity). — step_infectious_is
title: Timer-based I→S transition for SIS models (no immunity). — step_infectious_is
text: infectious agent, decrements timer 1. timer reaches 0 agent transitions directly back state S.

title: Timer-based I→S transition for SIS models (no immunity). — step_infectious_is
Timer-based I→S transition for SIS models (no immunity). — step_infectious_is
text: people LaserFrame agents.

title: Timer-based I→S transition for SIS models (no immunity). — step_infectious_is
text: fixed-state shorthand step_timer_expire()(people, , S).

what: Stochastic mortality step using a crude death rate. — step_mortality_cdr
title: Stochastic mortality step using a crude death rate. — step_mortality_cdr
text: living (non-D) agent, draws Bernoulli(cdr) death. Agents die state set D (-1) timer set 0. Dead agents remain frame — call $squash(people$state >= 0L) periodically compact.

title: Stochastic mortality step using a crude death rate. — step_mortality_cdr
Stochastic mortality step using a crude death rate. — step_mortality_cdr
text: people LaserFrame agents. cdr Crude death rate per agent per tick (probability [0, 1]).

title: Stochastic mortality step using a crude death rate. — step_mortality_cdr
text: agent's draw independent; Rayon assigns fixed slice worker thread. RNG thread-local (locking).

what: Timer-based R→S transition for waning-immunity models. — step_recovered_rs
title: Timer-based R→S transition for waning-immunity models. — step_recovered_rs
text: recovered agent, decrements timer 1. timer reaches 0 agent becomes susceptible (state S).

title: Timer-based R→S transition for waning-immunity models. — step_recovered_rs
Timer-based R→S transition for waning-immunity models. — step_recovered_rs
text: people LaserFrame agents.

title: Timer-based R→S transition for waning-immunity models. — step_recovered_rs
text: fixed-state shorthand step_timer_expire()(people, R, S).

what: Generalized timer-expiry transition into an absorbing (untimed) state. — step_timer_expire
title: Generalized timer-expiry transition into an absorbing (untimed) state. — step_timer_expire
text: agent currently from_state, decrements timer 1; timer reaches 0 agent moves to_state timer left 0. destination carries duration — "transition absorbing state" generalization (laser-generic's nb_timer_update), e.g. →S SIS, →R SIR, R→S waning.

title: Generalized timer-expiry transition into an absorbing (untimed) state. — step_timer_expire
Generalized timer-expiry transition into an absorbing (untimed) state. — step_timer_expire
text: people LaserFrame agents. from_state Integer state code agent must currently occupy eligible. to_state Integer state code agent moves timer expires.

title: Generalized timer-expiry transition into an absorbing (untimed) state. — step_timer_expire
text: engine behind step_infectious_is() (→S) step_recovered_rs() (R→S): step_timer_expire(people, laser_states()[[""]], laser_states()[["S"]]) exactly step_infectious_is(people). Required people properties: state, timer (integer).

what: Generalized timer-expiry transition into a state that has its own duration. — step_timer_expire_set
title: Generalized timer-expiry transition into a state that has its own duration. — step_timer_expire_set
text: agent currently from_state, decrements timer 1; timer reaches 0 agent moves to_state timer reset fresh per-agent draw duration_dist (rounded whole ticks, clamped minimum 1). "transition state duration timer" generalization (laser-generic's nb_timer_update_timer_set), e.g. E→SEIR →R waning SEIRS.

title: Generalized timer-expiry transition into a state that has its own duration. — step_timer_expire_set
Generalized timer-expiry transition into a state that has its own duration. — step_timer_expire_set
text: people LaserFrame agents. from_state Integer state code agent must currently occupy eligible. to_state Integer state code agent moves timer expires. duration_dist Distribution (e.g. dist_constant() dist_normal()) giving destination state's duration ticks; sampled per transitioning agent written timer.

title: Generalized timer-expiry transition into a state that has its own duration. — step_timer_expire_set
text: engine behind step_exposed_ei() (E→) step_infectious_ir() (→R): step_timer_expire_set(people, laser_states()[["E"]], laser_states()[[""]], inf_dist) exactly step_exposed_ei(people, inf_dist). RNG: thread-local (Pattern B) — Rayon worker draws thread_rng; single duration_dist handle shared across threads reference. Required people properties: state, timer (integer).

what: Stochastic S→E exposure step (SEIR kernel). — step_transmission_se
title: Stochastic S→E exposure step (SEIR kernel). — step_transmission_se
text: FOI computation parallelism step_transmission_si, newly exposed agents move state E timer set draw exp_dist (incubation period). Pair step_exposed_ei complete E→.

title: Stochastic S→E exposure step (SEIR kernel). — step_transmission_se
Stochastic S→E exposure step (SEIR kernel). — step_transmission_se
text: people LaserFrame agents. nodes LaserFrame patches/nodes. beta Transmission rate. exp_dist Distribution (e.g. dist_constant() dist_normal()) giving incubation period ticks; sampled per newly exposed agent written timer S→E (rounded whole ticks, clamped minimum 1).

title: Stochastic S→E exposure step (SEIR kernel). — step_transmission_se
text: Required people properties: state, node, timer (integer). Required nodes properties: N, (integer; overwritten).

what: Stochastic S→I transmission step (SI / SIR kernel). — step_transmission_si
title: Stochastic S→I transmission step (SI / SIR kernel). — step_transmission_si
text: Parallelism: active agent array split fixed chunks — one per Rayon worker thread — matching prange pattern. aggregation phase uses thread-local fold reduces element-wise summation. FOI phase runs chunk independently; RNG thread-local (rand::thread_rng()).

title: Stochastic S→I transmission step (SI / SIR kernel). — step_transmission_si
Stochastic S→I transmission step (SI / SIR kernel). — step_transmission_si
text: people LaserFrame agents. nodes LaserFrame patches/nodes. beta Transmission rate (force infection per infectious contact per tick). inf_dist Distribution (e.g. dist_constant() dist_normal()) giving infectious period ticks; sampled per newly infected agent written timer S→(rounded whole ticks, clamped minimum 1).

title: Stochastic S→I transmission step (SI / SIR kernel). — step_transmission_si
text: operations performed -place backing arrays; copies made. Node-level counts computed current agent states written nodes$(overwriting previous value). Required people properties: state (integer), node (integer, 0-based), timer (integer). Required nodes properties: N (integer, total population per node), (integer, overwritten).
